revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[沼津市]]大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = [[高龗神]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = [[文明]]元年（[[1469年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]大平（おおひら）にある[[神社]][http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は[[長泉町]]の[[割狐塚稲荷神社]]の[[兼務社]]となっている。 == 概要 == 沼津市の東端、[[静浦山地]]と[[狩野川]]に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の鷲頭山の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 [[式内社]]である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために[[文明]]元年（[[1469年]]）に伊予の神社から水神である[[高龗神]]を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（[[1481年]]）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に村社に列する。 == 祭神 == * [[高龗神]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[沼津市]]大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = [[高龗神]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = [[文明]]元年（[[1469年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Washizu Shrine'''|鷲頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 3 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[沼津市]]大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = [[高龗神]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = [[文明 (日本)|文明]]元年（[[1469年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]大平（おおひら）にある[[神社]][http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は[[長泉町]]の[[割狐塚稲荷神社]]の[[兼務社]]となっている。 == 概要 == 沼津市の東端、[[静浦山地]]と[[狩野川]]に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の[[鷲頭山]]の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 [[式内社]]である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために[[文明 (日本)|文明]]元年（[[1469年]]）に伊予の神社から水神である[[高龗神]]を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（[[1481年]]）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に村社に列する。 == 祭神 == * [[高龗神]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]大平（おおひら）にある[[神社]][http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は[[長泉町]]の[[割狐塚稲荷神社]]の[[兼務社]]となっている。 == 概要 == 沼津市の東端、[[静浦山地]]と[[狩野川]]に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の[[鷲頭山]]の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 [[式内社]]である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために[[文明 (日本)|文明]]元年（[[1469年]]）に伊予の神社から水神である[[高龗神]]を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（[[1481年]]）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 [[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に村社に列する。 == 祭神 == * [[高龗神]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|高龗神}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平（おおひら）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は{{jalink|長泉町|Q1204459}}の{{jalink|割狐塚稲荷神社|Q117463192}}の{{jalink|兼務社}}となっている。 == 概要 == 沼津市の東端、{{jalink|静浦山地|Q11663639}}と{{jalink|狩野川|Q6364188}}に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の{{jalink|鷲頭山|Q11676307}}の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 {{jalink|式内社}}である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために{{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}）に伊予の神社から水神である{{jalink|高龗神}}を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（{{jalink|1481年|Q6631}}）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（{{jalink|1875年|Q7764}}）に村社に列する。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|高龗神}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|高龗神}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平（おおひら）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は{{jalink|長泉町|Q1204459}}の{{jalink|割狐塚稲荷神社|Q117463192}}の{{jalink|兼務社}}となっている。 === 概要 === 沼津市の東端、{{jalink|静浦山地|Q11663639}}と{{jalink|狩野川|Q6364188}}に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の{{jalink|鷲頭山|Q11676307}}の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 {{jalink|式内社}}である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために{{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}）に伊予の神社から水神である{{jalink|高龗神}}を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（{{jalink|1481年|Q6631}}）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（{{jalink|1875年|Q7764}}）に村社に列する。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|高龗神}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|高龗神}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平（おおひら）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は{{jalink|長泉町|Q1204459}}の{{jalink|割狐塚稲荷神社|Q117463192}}の{{jalink|兼務社}}となっている。 === 概要 === 沼津市の東端、{{jalink|静浦山地|Q11663639}}と{{jalink|狩野川|Q6364188}}に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の{{jalink|鷲頭山|Q11676307}}の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 {{jalink|式内社}}である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために{{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}）に伊予の神社から水神である{{jalink|高龗神}}を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（{{jalink|1481年|Q6631}}）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（{{jalink|1875年|Q7764}}）に村社に列する。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|高龗神}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|高龗神}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平（おおひら）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は{{jalink|長泉町|Q1204459}}の{{jalink|割狐塚稲荷神社|Q117463192}}の{{jalink|兼務社}}となっている。 === 概要 === 沼津市の東端、{{jalink|静浦山地|Q11663639}}と{{jalink|狩野川|Q6364188}}に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の{{jalink|鷲頭山|Q11676307}}の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 {{jalink|式内社}}である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために{{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}）に伊予の神社から水神である{{jalink|高龗神}}を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（{{jalink|1481年|Q6631}}）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（{{jalink|1875年|Q7764}}）に村社に列する。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|高龗神}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|高龗神}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平（おおひら）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は{{jalink|長泉町|Q1204459}}の{{jalink|割狐塚稲荷神社|Q117463192}}の{{jalink|兼務社}}となっている。 === 概要 === 沼津市の東端、{{jalink|静浦山地|Q11663639}}と{{jalink|狩野川|Q6364188}}に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の{{jalink|鷲頭山|Q11676307}}の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 {{jalink|式内社}}である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために{{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}）に伊予の神社から水神である{{jalink|高龗神}}を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（{{jalink|1481年|Q6631}}）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（{{jalink|1875年|Q7764}}）に村社に列する。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|高龗神}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|高龗神}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平（おおひら）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は{{jalink|長泉町|Q1204459}}の{{jalink|割狐塚稲荷神社|Q117463192}}の{{jalink|兼務社}}となっている。 === 概要 === 沼津市の東端、{{jalink|静浦山地|Q11663639}}と{{jalink|狩野川|Q6364188}}に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の{{jalink|鷲頭山|Q11676307}}の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 {{jalink|式内社}}である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために{{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}）に伊予の神社から水神である{{jalink|高龗神}}を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（{{jalink|1481年|Q6631}}）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（{{jalink|1875年|Q7764}}）に村社に列する。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|高龗神}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鷲頭神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平1824 |位置 = 里宮：{{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|高龗神}} |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=鷲頭神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=鷲頭神社
(里宮)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''鷲頭神社'''（わしづじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}大平（おおひら）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 現在は{{jalink|長泉町|Q1204459}}の{{jalink|割狐塚稲荷神社|Q117463192}}の{{jalink|兼務社}}となっている。 === 概要 === 沼津市の東端、{{jalink|静浦山地|Q11663639}}と{{jalink|狩野川|Q6364188}}に挟まれた大平地区の南端、大平山の南麓の先にある小山（天満山）の山上に鎮座している。 またそこから南西の{{jalink|鷲頭山|Q11676307}}の山頂に奥宮として祠が建っている。 {{jalink|式内社}}である大朝神社の論社に挙げられることもあるが、治水・干ばつのための祈願をするために{{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}元年（{{jalink|1469年|Q6566}}）に伊予の神社から水神である{{jalink|高龗神}}を勧請したという創建の社伝が残っているため、有力視されていない[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - 玄松子[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - 延喜式神社の調査。 創建当初は現在地から少し南西の、鷲津山山麓の戸ケ谷の観音堂横に鎮座したとされるが、文明13年（{{jalink|1481年|Q6631}}）に鷲巣山山頂に遷座した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）に現在地である天満山山頂に遷座して天神社と村中の神社を合祀、明治8年（{{jalink|1875年|Q7764}}）に村社に列する。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|高龗神}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} |社格 = former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建 = {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{jalink|Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192}} in {{jalink|Nagaizumi|Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{jalink|Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639}} and the {{jalink|Kano River|Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{jalink|Mount Washizu|Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{jalink|1481|Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 7 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{jalink|1875|Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} |社格 = former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建 = {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{jalink|Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192}} in {{jalink|Nagaizumi|Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{jalink|Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639}} and the {{jalink|Kano River|Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{jalink|Mount Washizu|Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{jalink|1481|Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 7 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{jalink|1875|Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:need translation]]

= {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} |社格 = former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建 = {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{jalink|Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192}} in {{jalink|Nagaizumi|Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{jalink|Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639}} and the {{jalink|Kano River|Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{jalink|Mount Washizu|Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{jalink|1481|Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 7 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{jalink|1875|Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} |社格 = former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建 = {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{jalink|Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192}} in {{jalink|Nagaizumi|Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{jalink|Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639}} and the {{jalink|Kano River|Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{jalink|Mount Washizu|Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{jalink|1481|Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 7 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{jalink|1875|Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} |社格 = former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |創建 = {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{jalink|Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192}} in {{jalink|Nagaizumi|Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{jalink|Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639}} and the {{jalink|Kano River|Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{jalink|Mount Washizu|Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}} 1 ({{jalink|1469|Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{jalink|1481|Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 7 ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{jalink|1875|Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Takaokami-no-Kami}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

= {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill||lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill||lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill||lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192|1=Warikozuka Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459|1=Nagaizumi}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639|1=Shizuura Mountains}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188|1=Kano River}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307|1=Mount Washizu}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192|1=Warikozuka Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459|1=Nagaizumi}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639|1=Shizuura Mountains}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188|1=Kano River}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307|1=Mount Washizu}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192|1=Warikozuka Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459|1=Nagaizumi}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639|1=Shizuura Mountains}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188|1=Kano River}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307|1=Mount Washizu}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website {{DEFAULTSORT:わしつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192|1=Warikozuka Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459|1=Nagaizumi}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639|1=Shizuura Mountains}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188|1=Kano River}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307|1=Mount Washizu}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192|1=Warikozuka Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459|1=Nagaizumi}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639|1=Shizuura Mountains}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188|1=Kano River}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307|1=Mount Washizu}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192|1=Warikozuka Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459|1=Nagaizumi}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639|1=Shizuura Mountains}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188|1=Kano River}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307|1=Mount Washizu}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]
