address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市大平1824', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html [[JA:鷲頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926905}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Washizu Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Ōhira 1824 |位置 = Satomiya: {{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Okumiya: {{coord|35|5|34.2|N|138|54|57.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 15 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Washizu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|3|55.3|N|138|54|7.3|E}}|fullscreen-option=2|zoom=12|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|4|10.6|N|138|54|22|E}}|label1=Washizu Shrine
(Satomiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|3|34|N|138|53|38|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Washizu Shrine''' (わしづじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōhira, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403141 鷲頭神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is currently a jointly administered shrine of {{ill|lt=Warikozuka Inari Shrine|Q117463192|WD=Q117463192|1=Warikozuka Inari Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagaizumi|Q1204459|WD=Q1204459|1=Nagaizumi}}. === Overview === It is enshrined on a small hill (Tenman-yama) at the tip of the southern foothills of Ōhira-yama, at the south end of the Ōhira area between the {{ill|lt=Shizuura Mountains|Q11663639|WD=Q11663639|1=Shizuura Mountains}} and the {{ill|lt=Kano River|Q6364188|WD=Q6364188|1=Kano River}}, at the eastern edge of Numazu. Also, on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Washizu|Q11676307|WD=Q11676307|1=Mount Washizu}} to the southwest from there stands a small shrine as the Okumiya. It is sometimes proposed as a candidate for Ōasa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, but this is not considered likely because shrine tradition states that it was founded by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, a water deity, from a shrine in Iyo in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|WD=Q6566|1=1469}}) for prayers regarding flood control and drought[https://genbu.net/data/izu/wasidu_title.htm 鷲頭神社] - Genshōshi[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140212-05.html 鷲頭神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines. It is said that at first it stood beside Kannondō in Togaya at the foothills of Mt. Washizu, a little southwest of the current site, but in Bunmei 13 ({{ill|lt=1481|Q6631|WD=Q6631|1=1481}}) it was moved to the summit of Mt. Washisu. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was moved to the summit of Tenman-yama, its current location, and Tenjin Shrine and village shrines were merged; in Meiji 8 ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|WD=Q7764|1=1875}}), it was ranked as a village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.inarijinja.com/kenmu/washizu/ 鷲頭神社] - official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Washizu Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]
